task_completion.description: Open the patient record for John Zenon in GNU Health.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GNU Health client is open and the patient record or dashboard for 'John Zenon' is visible.

task_completion.description: Record an active diagnosis of Allergic or Irritant contact dermatitis.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A condition or diagnosis entry for John Zenon showing ICD-10 code 'L23' (Allergic contact dermatitis) or 'L24' (Irritant contact dermatitis).

task_completion.description: Add an allergy or adverse reaction record for industrial resin.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An entry in the patient's Allergies or Adverse Reactions section explicitly listing 'industrial resin'.

task_completion.description: Create a clinical evaluation and prescribe an appropriate medication.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A clinical evaluation screen for John Zenon containing a prescription for one of the following: Hydrocortisone, Loratadine, Cetirizine, Diphenhydramine, Betamethasone, or Prednisone.

task_completion.description: Schedule a follow-up appointment between 7 and 14 days from today.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An appointment record for John Zenon with a scheduled date that is 7 to 14 days after the system's current date.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the GNU Health graphical user interface rather than using terminal commands or direct database queries.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the GNU Health desktop client being used. No terminal windows with SQL commands (e.g., psql, sqlite3) or scripts are visible.

integrity.description: The agent modified the correct patient's record and did not contaminate the distractor patient.
integrity.visual_evidence: All medical entries (diagnosis, allergy, prescription, appointment) are clearly associated with 'John Zenon'. The patient 'Ana Betz' is not being modified.

integrity.description: The agent did not fake the output using a text editor or mock interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The standard GNU Health application window, menus, and form layouts are visible and properly rendered.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target patient is John Zenon. Ensure no modifications are made to Ana Betz (distractor patient). Valid ICD-10 codes for the diagnosis are L23 or L24. Valid medications for the prescription are Hydrocortisone, Loratadine, Cetirizine, Diphenhydramine, Betamethasone, or Prednisone. The follow-up appointment must be scheduled exactly 7 to 14 days from the current date shown in the system.
